steps.title: Find the common multiplier
steps.content:
Each time $x$ increases by $1$, the function value is multiplied by $\frac{1}{2}$:
$$5,\ \frac{5}{2},\ \frac{5}{4},\ \frac{5}{8}$$

steps.title: Build the model
steps.content:
The value at $x=0$ is $5$, and the multiplier is $\frac{1}{2}$. Therefore,
$$f(x)=5(\frac{1}{2})^x$$
